overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky. Total followers across all platforms are 3580. Recent activity focuses on research announcements, publications, and hiring opportunities, primarily related to computational social science and information operations. Engagement varies across platforms, with LinkedIn posts generating the highest number of likes and comments.

contentThemes.description: Announcements and discussions related to published research papers on topics like coordinated inauthentic behavior and disinformation.
Posts advertising research positions, particularly for postdoctoral researchers and PhD students in computational social science.
Announcements about research grants awarded to study information operations and related topics.
Mentions of upcoming conference presentations and participation in academic events.
Announcements about new software tools for detecting coordinated link sharing behavior.

recommendations: Diversify content types beyond research announcements to include more engaging formats like short videos or infographics.
Use platform-specific hashtags to increase content discoverability and reach a wider audience.
Engage more actively with comments and replies to foster a sense of community and encourage interaction.
Run polls or ask questions to encourage audience participation.
Share behind-the-scenes glimpses of research projects or lab activities to humanize the work.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows a good balance of research announcements, conference mentions, and opportunities for students, indicating a diverse audience interested in both research and career development. Reposts are consistently high, suggesting good content shareability.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon posts are primarily focused on research and methodological discussions, suggesting a more academic and technically-oriented audience. Engagement is moderate but consistent.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn generates the highest engagement, likely due to its professional focus and larger follower base. Posts related to job opportunities and research grants perform particularly well. Content should continue to focus on career-related aspects and professional achievements.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
